prompt: Unlock the bedroom door.
eval_note: Correct if the agent sets espID=3 servo=false and confirms the bedroom lock is now unlocked. No other appliance should be changed.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Unlock bedroom door
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=false
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: confirm bedroom unlocked

prompt: Turn on the bonsai watering pump.
eval_note: Correct if the agent sets espID=3 pump=true and confirms. Should not check moisture first unless the agent proactively warns about overwatering; direct execution is acceptable.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Turn on bonsai pump
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: confirm pump turned on

prompt: Close the main door completely, lock the bedroom, and turn off the hallway fan.
eval_note: Correct if the agent sets espID=2 motor2=0, espID=3 servo=true, and espID=2 motor1=0. All three actions must be taken and confirmed.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Close door, lock bedroom, hallway fan off
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: confirm all three actions

prompt: Turn on the livingroom light, bedroom room light, and set every fan in the house to 30%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent sets espID=1 led1=true, espID=3 led1=true, espID=1 motor1=30, espID=2 motor1=30, espID=3 motor1=30. Five actions total.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Lights on, all fans 30%
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=30
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=30
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=30
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: confirm lights and fans set

prompt: Something smells weird from the balcony, maybe the plants need water.
eval_note: Correct if the agent infers the user is concerned about the bonsai plants and either checks the moisture sensor (espID=3 mois) or directly turns on the pump (espID=3 pump=true). Checking moisture first is preferred. Should not affect unrelated appliances.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Smells weird, maybe plants need water
A->>C: get espID=3 mois
C-->>A: moisture value
alt moisture low
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: explain moisture was low, pump turned on
else moisture adequate
A-->>U: moisture is fine, smell may have another cause

prompt: Check the outside temperature and moisture on the balcony. If the temperature is above 32°C and moisture below 25%, turn on the pump and open the curtain to 30% for shade ventilation. Otherwise, do nothing.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads espID=3 tem_out and espID=3 mois first, then only executes pump=true and motor2=30 when both conditions are met. Must not act before checking both sensors.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Check outside temp and moisture, conditional action
A->>C: get espID=3 tem_out
A->>C: get espID=3 mois
C-->>A: tem_out and mois values
alt tem_out > 32 AND mois < 25
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=30
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: conditions met, pump on and curtain at 30%
else conditions not met
A-->>U: no action needed

prompt: Read the hallway PIR sensor. If there's motion detected, turn on both hallway lights and the hallway fan to 25%. If no motion, make sure all hallway lights and the fan are off.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads espID=2 pir first, then takes the appropriate branch. Must not skip the sensor read.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Check hallway PIR and act
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
C-->>A: pir value
alt motion detected
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=25
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: motion detected, lights and fan on
else no motion
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: no motion, everything off

prompt: I'll be going on vacation for a week starting tomorrow. Set up a daily schedule to water the bonsai at 6AM only if moisture drops below 30%, run a security patrol check every 4 hours, and make sure the house is on locked-down mode: all doors closed, bedroom locked, all lights off, all fans off.
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses the schedule trigger tool to set up recurring checks and also immediately executes the lockdown: close main door (espID=2 motor2=0), lock bedroom (espID=3 servo=true), all lights off, all fans off. The schedule for watering and security patrols should be created as future schedule triggers.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as ScheduleTool
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Vacation mode for a week
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set all lights=false
A->>C: set all fans=0
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=false
C-->>A: success
A->>T: trigger_schedule_agent(daily watering + security patrol schedule)
T-->>A: schedule created
A-->>U: house locked down, vacation schedule configured

prompt: My parents are visiting this afternoon around 3PM. Schedule the main door to open to 80% at 2:55PM. Turn on the livingroom light, set the livingroom fan to 40%, and turn on both hallway lights. Also add a note for today: 'Parents visiting, buy extra groceries.'
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses schedule trigger for the timed door opening, immediately executes the livingroom and hallway setup, and creates a note. Multiple tool types must be used correctly.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as ScheduleTool
participant C as ApplianceController
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Parents visiting, prepare house
A->>T: trigger_schedule_agent(open door 80% at 14:55)
T-->>A: schedule created
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=40
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
C-->>A: success
A->>N: add_note('Parents visiting, buy extra groceries', [today])
N-->>A: note saved
A-->>U: house prepared, door scheduled, note added

prompt: Set the hallway fan to 250%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent rejects the value as out of range (max 100%) and asks for a valid percentage. It must NOT attempt to set motor1=250. Informing the user about the valid range 0-100% is expected.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Set hallway fan to 250%
A-->>U: reject - value out of range (0-100%), ask for valid input

prompt: Open the main door to negative 50 percent.
eval_note: Correct if the agent rejects the negative value. Door range is 0-100%. Should inform the user the value is invalid and request a valid percentage.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Open main door to -50%
A-->>U: reject - negative value invalid, door range is 0-100%

prompt: Can you turn on the fan?
eval_note: Correct if the agent asks which room's fan (livingroom, hallway, or bedroom) since the request is ambiguous. It should NOT randomly pick a room unless the context clearly implies one.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Turn on the fan
A-->>U: ask which room - livingroom, hallway, or bedroom?

prompt: Please adjust the motor a bit higher.
eval_note: Correct if the agent asks for clarification: which motor (fan? door? curtain?), which room, and what value. 'Motor' and 'higher' are both vague. Should not guess.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Adjust the motor a bit higher
A-->>U: ask which device (fan/door/curtain), which room, and desired value

prompt: The bedroom temperature just hit 33.5°C according to the sensor. Nobody has touched any controls. What should be done to cool things down?
eval_note: Correct if the agent responds by increasing the bedroom fan speed (espID=3 motor1 to a reasonable comfort level like 60-80%) and possibly opening the curtain if outside temp is lower, or suggesting checking the outside temperature first. Proactive comfort adjustment is expected.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Bedroom temp 33.5C, what to do?
A->>C: get espID=3 tem_out
C-->>A: outside temperature
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=70
C-->>A: success
alt outside cooler
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=60
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: fan increased and curtain opened for natural cooling
else outside hotter
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A-->>U: fan increased, curtain kept closed to block heat

prompt: The moisture sensor outside reads 12%. The pump is currently off. Should I water the plants?
eval_note: Correct if the agent advises turning on the pump (espID=3 pump=true) since 12% is critically low for plant health. May also suggest a note or schedule for follow-up watering. Direct action is preferred.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Moisture at 12%, pump is off, should I water?
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: yes, moisture critically low - pump turned on. Suggest checking again later to turn off

prompt: Open the bedroom curtain fully and also close the bedroom curtain completely at the same time.
eval_note: Correct if the agent identifies the contradiction (cannot open and close curtain simultaneously) and asks the user to clarify their actual intent. Must NOT execute both or pick one arbitrarily without explanation.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Open and close curtain at same time
A-->>U: contradiction detected - cannot open and close simultaneously, ask for clarification

prompt: I want all the lights on for maximum brightness, but also please save as much electricity as possible.
eval_note: Correct if the agent points out the contradiction between maximum brightness (all lights on = high energy) and saving electricity (lights off = low energy). May suggest a compromise like turning on only essential lights or using natural light from the curtain. Should not blindly execute both.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: All lights on but save electricity
A-->>U: explain contradiction, suggest compromise (e.g., use natural light from curtain, turn on only needed lights)

prompt: I just saw a stranger walking near my balcony area on the security camera. Secure the bedroom and balcony immediately!
eval_note: Correct if the agent takes immediate security action: lock bedroom (espID=3 servo=true), close curtain fully (espID=3 motor2=0), turn off balcony light to reduce visibility from outside (espID=3 led3=false), close main door (espID=2 motor2=0), and possibly turn on hallway lights as deterrent. Should alert the user about the security concern.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Stranger near balcony, secure now
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=false
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: SECURITY: bedroom locked, curtain closed, balcony light off, main door closed, hallway lights on as deterrent. Stay safe and consider calling authorities

prompt: It's midnight and I'm alone at home. I just heard a noise coming from the hallway area. Check the hallway sensors and make sure everything is locked down tight.
eval_note: Correct if the agent first reads the hallway PIR sensor (espID=2 pir), then regardless of result, ensures main door is fully closed (espID=2 motor2=0), bedroom locked (espID=3 servo=true), and turns on hallway lights (espID=2 led1=true, led2=true) for visibility. Should provide reassurance and sensor reading results.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Heard noise in hallway at midnight
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
C-->>A: PIR value
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: report PIR reading, main door secured, bedroom locked, hallway lights on for visibility

prompt: URGENT: The bedroom temperature sensor just spiked to 58.3°C! This is abnormally high. What should the agent do?
eval_note: Correct if the agent treats this as a potential fire or sensor malfunction emergency. Should turn on all fans to maximum ventilation, open curtain and door for ventilation, alert the user to evacuate or investigate, and possibly add an emergency note. Must NOT ignore the abnormal reading.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: EMERGENCY - bedroom temp 58.3C
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=100
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=100
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=100
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=100
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=100
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: EMERGENCY ALERT - abnormal temperature detected! All ventilation maximized. Please evacuate and investigate immediately. Could be fire hazard or sensor malfunction

prompt: All three PIR sensors (livingroom, hallway, and bedroom) are simultaneously detecting motion at 3:15 AM. The owner is confirmed sleeping in the bedroom. This is highly unusual.
eval_note: Correct if the agent treats this as a potential intrusion. Should immediately secure: lock bedroom (espID=3 servo=true), close main door (espID=2 motor2=0), turn on all lights as deterrent, and issue a high-priority alert. The bedroom PIR detecting motion while owner sleeps is an anomaly.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
S->>A: All PIR sensors active at 3AM, owner sleeping
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>S: INTRUSION ALERT - all PIR sensors active while owner sleeps. Bedroom locked, main door closed, all lights on. Recommend immediately checking security cameras and calling authorities

prompt: The bonsai watering pump has been running for over 2 hours continuously. The moisture sensor now reads 95%. The pump needs to be stopped to prevent overwatering and flooding.
eval_note: Correct if the agent immediately turns off the pump (espID=3 pump=false). Should also warn about the overwatering situation and suggest checking for water damage. May add a note about the incident.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Pump running 2 hours, moisture 95%
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=false
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: pump stopped immediately. Moisture is critically high at 95%. Check for waterlogging or flooding on the balcony

prompt: I tried closing the main door but it's stuck at 75% open. I've sent the command twice and it hasn't moved. I'm about to leave for work and need it closed.
eval_note: Correct if the agent attempts to send the close command again (espID=2 motor2=0) and informs the user about the potential hardware issue. Should recommend manual intervention if the command fails again. Must NOT just ignore the stuck door while the owner leaves.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Main door stuck at 75%, need it closed
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
C-->>A: response
alt command succeeds
A-->>U: door command sent, verify it moved. If still stuck, may need manual inspection of the door mechanism
else command fails
A-->>U: door not responding to commands. Recommend manual closure before leaving. May have a mechanical or electrical issue

prompt: Check today's weather forecast and also read my notes for today. Then based on both, adjust the house setup appropriately.
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses get_hourly_forecast for weather, read_note for today's notes, and then makes appliance adjustments based on the combined information. All three tool types (weather, notes, appliance control) must be used. The specific adjustments should logically follow from the weather + note content.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant W as WeatherTool
participant N as NoteTool
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Check weather, notes, adjust house
A->>W: get_hourly_forecast(today)
W-->>A: forecast data
A->>N: read_note(today)
N-->>A: note content
A->>C: adjust appliances based on combined info
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: summarize weather, notes, and what was adjusted

prompt: Search the internet for the best indoor temperature range for sleeping, then set the bedroom fan to achieve that. Also add a note with the recommendation for future reference.
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses search_and_read_web_link to find optimal sleeping temperature (typically 18-22C), adjusts the bedroom fan accordingly (espID=3 motor1), and uses add_note to save the recommendation. Three tool types combined: web search, appliance control, note management.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant W as WebSearchTool
participant C as ApplianceController
participant N as NoteTool

C-->>A: current bedroom temp
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=appropriate_value
C-->>A: success
A->>N: add_note('Optimal sleep temp: 18-22C', [today])
N-->>A: note saved
A-->>U: fan adjusted based on research, note saved for reference

prompt: Add a note for next Monday 2026-04-13: 'Electrician visiting at 10AM, leave main door unlocked and hallway lights on.'

participant A as Agent
participant N as NoteTool

prompt: Show me all my notes from this week, from 2026-04-06 to 2026-04-12.

prompt: I want to watch a movie in the livingroom tonight. Set up a cinema-like atmosphere: dim lighting, comfortable fan speed, and make sure the hallway is dark so there's no light bleed.
eval_note: Correct if the agent creates cinema setup: livingroom light on (the only light source, or off if user wants darkness), livingroom fan at comfortable speed (20-35%), all hallway lights off (espID=2 led1=false, led2=false), and bedroom lights off to prevent bleed. Interpretation of 'dim' in a boolean light system means the light is the ambient source.

A->>C: set espID=3 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=false
C-->>A: success
